title: DDTrace Scope & Span Lifecycle

steps: [if span instanceof DDTrace\Span AND ObjectKVStore has 'ddtrace_scope_activated' key] → ObjectKVStore::put(span.internalSpan, 'ddtrace_scope_activated', true)
Call reconcileInternalAndUserland() — sync C-extension spans into userland stack
[if span is NOT DDTrace\Span] → return this scope (pure userland span, always active)
[if span IS DDTrace\Span] → check ObjectKVStore.get(span.internalSpan, 'ddtrace_scope_activated', default=true)
Search for scope in $scopes[] by identity
[if span instanceof DDTrace\Span AND ObjectKVStore has key] → ObjectKVStore::put(span.internalSpan, 'ddtrace_scope_activated', false)
Search for scope in $hostRootScopes[] by identity
Call scope.close() on each → triggers span.finish() + deactivate()
Walk $scopes[] from top, remove any scope whose span.isFinished() == true
→ build SpanContext(traceId, internalRootSpan.id, parentId from rootContext)
→ create Scope wrapping new Span(internalRootSpan, context), finishSpanOnClose=false
Walk active_span() chain (C-ext) downward from current active span
Stop when span.id matches the known top scope's spanId
For each gap span: create Scope(new Span(span, SpanContext), finishSpanOnClose=true)
Collect new scopes in reverse order (oldest first)
Set parentContext links on new scopes to chain correctly
Return newest scope added (or existing topScope if no gaps found)
Return first element of $scopes[] (root scope) or null if empty

key_relationships: ScopeManager composes Scope (creates and owns $scopes[] stack)
Scope holds reference back to ScopeManager (for deactivation on close)
ScopeManager uses ObjectKVStore to mark DDTrace\Span internalSpan with 'ddtrace_scope_activated' flag
ScopeManager calls C-extension functions: root_span(), active_span(), trace_id()
branching_conditions: finishSpanOnClose: bool — controls whether Scope::close() finishes the span
span instanceof DDTrace\Span — distinguishes internal (C-ext) spans from pure userland spans
ObjectKVStore 'ddtrace_scope_activated' key presence — only set for spans that were activated via userland ScopeManager
span.context.isHostRoot() — determines if scope is tracked in hostRootScopes for distributed tracing cleanup
scopes stack empty + root_span() available — triggers bootstrapping of root scope from C-extension
important_notes: Two-layer architecture: userland PHP scopes ($scopes[] stack) vs internal C-extension spans (active_span/root_span). reconcileInternalAndUserland() bridges the two.
reconcileInternalAndUserland() is called by getActive(), getPrimaryRoot(), getTopScope() — NOT by activate/deactivate.
Gaps between userland scope stack and C-ext active_span chain are auto-filled with synthetic Scope wrappers (finishSpanOnClose=true).
hostRootScopes tracks distributed tracing root spans; ScopeManager::close() terminates them all at request end.
DEFAULT_FINISH_SPAN_ON_CLOSE = true (interface constant); callers can opt out by passing false to activate().
Calling Scope::close() more than once is undefined behavior per interface contract.
ObjectKVStore uses false (not null) to mark a span as deactivated; null means the key was never set (i.e., not a userland-activated span).
